researcher_profile.name: Prof. Dr. Gloria Gross
researcher_profile.title: Associate Professor of Maritime Archaeology
researcher_profile.specialisation: Medieval North Sea and Baltic maritime trade; submerged site survey methods; multibeam sonar archaeological classification

project.title: North Frisian Medieval Wreck Survey: Phase 3 Excavation and Analysis
project.description: Systematic archaeological investigation of Site NF-7, a Hanseatic merchant vessel of c.1350-1420 in the North Frisian Islands. Phase 3 completed June-August 2025: 46 dive pairs, 84 finds recovered and transferred to Hamburg Maritime Museum. Target T08 identified for Phase 4 investigation.

outputs.output: Gross, G. and Whitfield, J. (2026) Multibeam Sonar as Primary Discovery Tool in Coastal Archaeological Surveys. Nordic Journal of Maritime Archaeology, 52(1), pp.47-71.
North Sea Coastal Heritage Annual Consortium, Hamburg Maritime Museum, November 2025

postgraduate_researchers.researcher: Anja Bergmann - clinker planking and hull construction analysis

project.title: North Sea Coastal Heritage Mapping Survey: Schleswig-Holstein Coastal Zone
project.funder: Hamburg Maritime Museum and Schleswig-Holstein State Heritage Authority
project.co_pi: Dr. Frida Moe, Hamburg Maritime Museum
project.description: Four-year systematic MBES survey programme covering approximately 23 km2 of the Schleswig-Holstein coastal zone. Year 3 (2025) completed: 74% coverage, 187 features logged, 28 confirmed anthropogenic. Delivering final GIS feature catalogue to heritage authority.

outputs.output: NF-7 GIS data package transferred to HMM (October 2025)
Joint paper for International Journal of Nautical Archaeology (submitted Q4 2026)

project.title: Climate Change, Accelerated Erosion, and the Archaeology of the Submerged North Sea Heritage Zone
project.co_pi: Prof. James Whitfield, Caledonia Maritime University
project.description: Five-year programme investigating climate change impacts on submerged heritage in the North Sea. Seven work packages covering survey of 14 high-risk sites, sediment analysis, oceanographic monitoring, and development of quantified risk model for organic site degradation. Outputs to inform EU Cultural Heritage Action Plan.

project.title: Hanseatic Routes Beneath the German Bight: A Maritime Archaeology of Trade and Technology, 1200-1600
project.funder: Internal research time allocation, Nordmeer Maritime University
project.description: Academic monograph synthesising 20 years of North Sea maritime archaeology research. Draft Chapter 1 complete (October 2025). Contracted with Brill Academic Publishers for 2028 publication.

project.title: Ships of the Hanse: Trade, Technology, and the Medieval North Sea
project.description: Major temporary exhibition at Hamburg Maritime Museum drawing on NF-7 finds and wider Hanseatic maritime archaeology. Includes reconstructed section of NF-7 hull, interactive cargo identification displays, and Frisian community heritage component. Subject to HMM board approval.

publication.title: Multibeam Sonar as Primary Discovery Tool in Coastal Archaeological Surveys: A North Sea Methodology

publication.authors: Gross, G., Moe, F., and Whitfield, J.
publication.title: NF-7 North Frisian Islands: Interim Report on Phase 1-2 Investigation of a Hanseatic Wreck Site
Community Archaeology and Intangible Heritage in Frisian Coastal Communities
Clinker Construction in the German Bight: Timber Species and Trade Routes
